name: Company Pot Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
2. Heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat.
3. Brown the roast in hot oil; remove it from the pot and set aside.
4. In the same pot, mix together the wine, garlic, carrots, onions, sour cream, salt, pepper, and thyme.
5. Return the roast to the pot.
6. Cover and bake at 325°F until the roast is tender (about 3½ hours), turning it a couple of times during baking.
7. Remove the roast and vegetables to a heated platter; keep them warm while preparing the gravy.
8. Skim any fat from the liquid in the pot.
9. In a covered jar, combine the water and flour. Stir this mixture into the hot liquid until smooth, stirring constantly.
10. Heat the gravy over medium heat for about 1 minute to ensure it's heated through.
11. Add lemon juice; stir well and cook for an additional minute.
12. Slice the roast thinly and serve with the gravy.
Note: Beef bottom round or boneless chuck eye roast can be substituted for the rolled rump roast.
